author: Sofia Martínez
title: Internal Linking Strategy for AI-Generated Content Clusters
description: How to structure internal links when you're publishing 3-5x more content with AI. Real framework that prevents thin content penalties and builds topical authority.

sections.p: We're seeing SMBs publish 40-60% more content in 2026 than they did two years ago, mostly powered by Claude or ChatGPT. But more content doesn't equal better rankings—not without a deliberate internal linking strategy. Google's March 2024 core update specifically targeted pages with weak topical relationships and shallow inbound link equity. If you're using AI to generate content, you need a system for connecting those pages so Google understands they're part of a cohesive knowledge base, not a content farm.

We structure AI-generated content in three layers: pillar pages (2,000–3,000 words, one per topic cluster), hub pages (1,200–1,500 words, subtopic overviews), and support pages (800–1,000 words, specific variations or use cases). Each layer has a different linking purpose. Pillar pages receive links from *every* hub and support page in their cluster. Hub pages link back to the pillar and forward to 3-5 support pages. Support pages link up to their hub and sideways to 1-2 related support pages. This prevents the "link bloat" problem where you're linking indiscriminately from every page to every page.

list: Pillar page: 12–15 inbound links per cluster (from hubs and support pages only)
Hub page: 6–8 inbound links (from pillar + related hub pages)
Support page: 2–4 inbound links (from hub + adjacent support pages)
Cross-cluster links: 1 per page (to related clusters only, not random)

sections.h2: Anchor Text Strategy When You Have 40+ Pages

sections.p: This is where AI content breaks most internal linking strategies. When you have dozens of similar pages, anchor text becomes messy fast. We use a rule: 60% branded/exact match anchors (e.g., "AI marketing for plumbers"), 30% long-tail keyword anchors (e.g., "how to implement AI customer retention"), 10% brand-only or "this article" anchors. For support pages, we lock anchor text to a specific format: [problem statement + solution keyword]. Example: "automate customer follow-up sequences" instead of the vaguer "read our guide." This consistency helps AI content pages reinforce each other's topical focus.

sections.quote: When you're publishing 50 pages a quarter instead of 5, your internal linking becomes your topical authority engine. Mess it up and you'll see 30% more thin content warnings in Search Console.

sections.h2: Preventing Dilution: The Cluster Integrity Check

sections.p: A mistake we see: linking support pages across clusters too liberally. You end up confusing Google about what your topical focus actually is. We use a monthly audit—takes about 2 hours for a 100-page site—where we map every internal link and count how many pages in each cluster receive links from *outside* their cluster. If more than 15% of your internal links escape the cluster, you're diluting authority. For a local service business with 60 AI-generated location and service combination pages, that's the difference between ranking for 12 competitive keywords versus 3.

Use a simple Google Sheet: cluster name, pillar URL, number of hub pages, number of support pages, number of inbound links from outside cluster, number of outbound links to outside cluster. Recalculate every 30 days. If external links exceed 15%, merge clusters or consolidate support pages.

sections.h2: Why Linking Matters More With AI Content

sections.p: AI can generate 800-word pages on "AI for chiropractors," "AI for dental practices," and "AI for physical therapy clinics" in 30 minutes. The content is competent. But without internal linking structure, Google sees three orphaned pages competing with each other instead of three pages supporting a pillar on "AI for health practices." We've measured this: sites that implemented tier-based internal linking after publishing AI content saw 34% faster indexation of new support pages and 18% higher rankings for cluster pillar pages within 8 weeks. Sites that didn't saw flat or declining rankings despite adding 3x more content.
